response.justification: This candidate is a full-stack web and platform engineer whose background is fundamentally misaligned with the role. The resume shows only ~6 months of C++ experience (vs. 5–10 years required), no Windows platform development, zero financial services or capital markets domain exposure, and a 10-year career trajectory moving entirely away from C++ toward TypeScript, Node.js, and scripting languages. While technically strong in general software engineering, the candidate lacks all three critical mandatory competencies.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (10+ years) demonstrating consistent craftsmanship, TDD practices, and architectural thinking
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing abilities evidenced by workshops, public talks, and mentoring roles
Proven ability to build scalable systems handling high throughput (millions of requests, paper mails) and complex infrastructure
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014–2015) versus mandatory 5–10 years requirement
No financial services, investment banking, capital markets, or fixed-income domain knowledge; career has focused on web, GIS, media, and automation platforms
Career trajectory for past decade shows sustained shift away from C++ toward TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang; no recent or deep SQL Server/Oracle experience mentioned
